repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Info taken from #18393

- [ ] Add this metadata into the docs

comment_data.body:
could we use the existing `@see` handling?
```suggestion
    see = { '|bit.band()|' }
```

Potentially. I'll let you make the final call on that.

The intention was to make the lua alternative the first line of the doc. I think in 2026 users should be strongly pushed away from using a lot of `vim.fn.*`.

I'll finish the patch with what I had in mind, then we can bikeshed some more.
Duplicated text
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1470,7 +1562,13 @@ ctxsize()                                                            *ctxsize()*
                   (`any`)
 
 cursor({lnum}, {col} [, {off}])                                       *cursor()*
+                Lua: Prefer |nvim_win_set_cursor()| with `{lnum, col - 1}`;
+                `off`, `curswant`, and failure semantics differ.
+
 cursor({list})
+                Lua: Prefer |nvim_win_set_cursor()| with `{lnum, col - 1}`;
+                `off`, `curswant`, and failure semantics differ.

comment_data.body:
off topic: since `runtime/doc/vimfn.txt` is generated, there is not really any reason for using tab indent here.

so either we should change to spaces in a followup, or these "Lua:" lines should be tab-indented?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -13,6 +13,8 @@ For functions grouped by what they are used for see |function-list|.
 1. Details					*vimscript-functions-details*
 
 abs({expr})                                                              *abs()*
+                Lua: Prefer |math.abs()|.
+
 		Return the absolute value of {expr}.  When {expr} evaluates to

comment_data.body:
this doesn't actually work in lua, though it should:

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/28437
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -85,6 +93,9 @@ and({expr}, {expr})                                                      *and()*
                   (`integer`)
 
 api_info()                                                          *api_info()*
+                Lua: Prefer |nvim_get_api_info()| (second item of the returned

comment_data.body: any objection to naming it `see_lua`, so it is mutually discoverable with `see` ?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -26,6 +32,10 @@
 --- @field lua? false Do not render type information
 --- @field tags? string[] Extra tags
 --- @field data? string Used by gen_eval.lua
+---
+--- Recommended alternatives if calling from Lua
+--- false is not applicable in Lua
+--- @field lua_alts? string[]|false

comment_data.body: see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/33313#discussion_r3017711364 (pun intended :)
nope, will change it.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -26,6 +32,10 @@
 --- @field lua? false Do not render type information
 --- @field tags? string[] Extra tags
 --- @field data? string Used by gen_eval.lua
+---
+--- Recommended alternatives if calling from Lua
+--- false is not applicable in Lua
+--- @field lua_alts? string[]|false

comment_data.body:
a 1 line example output would help give intuition about each of these functions. 

```suggestion
--- Example: "..."
local function join_with_conjunction(items, conjunction)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -261,6 +261,70 @@ local function norm_text(x, special)
   )
 end
 
+--- @param items string[]
+--- @param conjunction string
+--- @return string
+local function join_with_conjunction(items, conjunction)

comment_data.body: Really?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +593,8 @@ browsedir({title}, {initdir})                                      *browsedir()*
                   (`0|1`)
 
 bufadd({name})                                                        *bufadd()*
+		Lua: Prefer |nvim_create_buf()| with |nvim_buf_set_name()|.
+

comment_data.body: I don't think so...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1815,6 +1917,8 @@ empty({expr})                                                          *empty()*
                   (`integer`)
 
 environ()                                                            *environ()*
+		Lua: Prefer |vim.env|.

comment_data.body: some of these are more like "see also" rather than "preferred", but maybe we can adjust in a followup.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +593,8 @@ browsedir({title}, {initdir})                                      *browsedir()*
                   (`0|1`)
 
 bufadd({name})                                                        *bufadd()*
+		Lua: Prefer |nvim_create_buf()| with |nvim_buf_set_name()|.
+

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
      "|nvim_buf_get_offset()| with `lnum - 1`; 'fileformat' and invalid-line semantics differ",
```
"full-mode behavior differs" is a bit confusing.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6926,6 +7227,9 @@ mkdir({name} [, {flags} [, {prot}]])                              *mkdir()* *E73
                   (`integer`)
 
 mode([{expr}])                                                          *mode()*
+		Lua: Prefer |nvim_get_mode()|; use the `.mode` field for the
+		mode string, and note that full-mode behavior differs.

comment_data.body: Aren't strings and blobs the same in Lua?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -8114,6 +8459,9 @@ rename({from}, {to})                                                  *rename()*
                   (`integer`)
 
 repeat({expr}, {count})                                               *repeat()*
+		Lua: Prefer |string.rep()| for strings; lists and blobs need

comment_data.body: Multigrid behavior differs. `winwidth()` gets the inner width. `nvim_win_get_width()` gets the outer width.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -12353,6 +12897,9 @@ winsaveview()                                                    *winsaveview()*
                   (`vim.fn.winsaveview.ret`)
 
 winwidth({nr})                                                      *winwidth()*
+		Lua: Prefer |nvim_win_get_width()| for valid windows; invalid
+		IDs error instead of returning -1.

comment_data.body: Multigrid and winbar behavior differs. `winheight()` gets the inner height. `nvim_win_get_height()` gets the outer height (e.g. it includes winbar unlike `winheight()`).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -12180,6 +12716,9 @@ windowsversion()                                              *windowsversion()*
                   (`string`)
 
 winheight({nr})                                                    *winheight()*
+		Lua: Prefer |nvim_win_get_height()| for valid windows; invalid
+		IDs error instead of returning -1.

comment_data.body: You make it seem like it obvious. Could you elaborate just a little.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1815,6 +1917,8 @@ empty({expr})                                                          *empty()*
                   (`integer`)
 
 environ()                                                            *environ()*
+		Lua: Prefer |vim.env|.

comment_data.body: `vim.env` is a metatable so you cannot iterate over its keys. And `uv.os_environ()` will be a exact alternative anyway.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1815,6 +1917,8 @@ empty({expr})                                                          *empty()*
                   (`integer`)
 
 environ()                                                            *environ()*
+		Lua: Prefer |vim.env|.

comment_data.body:
nit/nonblocker, what is meant by "real", could just drop that or does it mean "full"? :)
```suggestion
environ()                                                            *environ()*
		Lua: Prefer |uv.os_environ()| for the full environment table;
		|vim.env| for keyed access.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1815,6 +1915,9 @@ empty({expr})                                                          *empty()*
                   (`integer`)
 
 environ()                                                            *environ()*
+		Lua: Prefer |uv.os_environ()| for a real environment table;
+		|vim.env| for keyed access.

comment_regular_data.body: It might be worth mentioning the existence of Lua alternatives in the notes at the top of `:h function-list` as well, since `:h function-list` serves as a quick reference.

title: feat(doc): document Lua alternative for vim.fn
